text:
Alice Nguyen: Kicking off private on-prem rollout v3.4.0 to cluster oak-2 in the maintenance window now. FYI deploybot autosuppress is ON for scheduled backups.
DeployBot: [system] Canary slice started for v3.4.0 on oak-2. Metrics: p95=180ms -> 520ms, error-rate=0.8% (threshold 0.5%). Auto-rollback suppressed due to maintenance_flag=true (backup job overlap).
Ben Carter: Seeing 5xx spike from oak-2-app-1 in logs, correlation with backup window looks strong.
Carol Singh: If backup is causing IO contention we might be getting a false positive. Should we lift autosuppress and let the system rollback, or do a manual pin/rollback?
Dana Lopez: Oncall for oak-2 here — checking node metrics. Disk IO for node-3 at 95%, backup id bk-2026-03-22-1 running since 6790012000.
Eve Martinez: Recommend manual isolation: shift traffic off oak-2 app nodes and pin private deployment to v3.3.9 until mornin...

text:
Alice Wang: heads up — noticed higher 95th latency on canary traffic after router patch. runtime still on v2.3.1, router bumped to v2.4.0 earlier today.
Ben Carter: ugh, which patch? the header-split one? :thinking_face: 
Alice Wang: yeah header-split + conn-pool tweak. console and api are on v2.3.x pinned by the rollout. router autoshift landed in prod.
Carol Nguyen: deploy-bot reported successful wave for router at 6161616100.
deploy-bot: wave completed: router v2.4.0 -> 100% (no errors). logs: /deploys/6161616100
Dan R.: how big is the 95th bump? numbers?
Alice Wang: from ~120ms -> ~210ms for tokenization-heavy requests. p50 unaffected.
Erin S.: could be header parsing change moving some work into runtime path due to header normalization. runtime now handles extra validation.
Ben Carter: we planned compatibility but forgot the runtime schema bump — oops. Is there a quick mitigation...

text:
Ethan Park: Quick preflight for tonight's deploy (01:00 UTC). Draft RN + escrow rollback owner below. Need ops + compliance ack.

Noah Singh: looking. what's being pinned?

Ethan Park: pinning model rev-2026-04-10, fallback rev-2026-03-30. Also shipping a jitter fix for batching and a tiny change to embedding headers (adds `client_request_id`).

Nina Gomez: embedding header change is additive; SDKs fine. I'll notify SDK leads and include a snippet.

Noah Singh: rollback triggers proposal: auto-pause if error-rate > 2% OR embedding invalids > 3%. manual rollback if p99 latency +10% for 10m or any OOMs on dedicated lanes.

Ethan Park: agreed. Add immediate rollback if any cold-start GPU memory spikes lead to failed initializations.

Nina Gomez: DeployBot please stage 8% canary at 01:00 UTC, automatic pause at 01:20 UTC for checks.

text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — need to push hotfix v2.3.1 to staging -> prod today, routing policy change included. Can we request a short change-window override?
Ravi Patel: What’s the canary plan? low-latency customers on Dedicated will need careful routing.
Ava Chen: Start 3% for 15m, then 20% for 30m, then ramp to 100%. Rollout config: 
```yaml
service: inference-api
model: text-gen-v2.3.1
steps:
 - canary: 3%
 duration: 15m
 - canary: 20%
 duration: 30m
 - full: 100%
guards:
 - name: latency
 threshold: 200ms
 - name: error_rate
 threshold: 1%
```
Maya Lopez: We’re in a scheduled maintenance window tomorrow morning but not today — why override?
Ava Chen: Customer-facing regression found in routing policy that breaks long-session reconnects. Need immediate rollout to mitigate.
Jordan (oncall): @Maya @Ravi I can take first watch. Ops telemetry shows increased reconnect errors on canary traff...

text:
Connor O'Brien: heads up, release train next week includes the auto-rollback-on-SLO-breach bits behind flags. want to lock staged enablement + checklist today so we don't wing it in prod.

Talia Benson: +1. can you drop the proposed phase plan here? i'll mirror it in ENG-4850.

Connor O'Brien: proposal (rough):
1) dev + staging: flag ON by default, all internal routes ok
2) prod: flag OFF globally, enable allowlist for 1 internal route in us-east only
3) expand to 2nd region + 1 hosted tier route
4) dedicated/private opt-in only (support-driven) until we have 2 weeks clean

Connor O'Brien: key question: do we have 1 or 2 flags? (eval logic vs action execution)

Jada Williams: controller side we have `rollouts.slo_eval_enabled` and `rollouts.auto_action_enabled`. eval can run + emit audit events even if action disabled.

text:
Connor O'Brien: Requesting a temporary release freeze for serving runtime rollouts until perf-canary signals stabilize + we finish threshold tuning. We had 2 warning spikes yesterday + 1 near-page this AM that look like baseline drift / ingestion lag, not real perf.

Connor O'Brien: Proposal:
- Freeze: runtime + kernel rollouts to prod (hosted + dedicated) for 48h
- Allowed: Sev1/Sev2 fixes w/ explicit signoff from SRE + runtime TL
- Goal: reduce overlapping changes while we tune min-samples/dampening + region/SKU thresholds

Connor O'Brien: Context links:
- Incident thread: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/incidents/p1769034455
- Canary baseline drift discussion: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/eng-runtime/p1768604412
- Current rollout schedule sheet: https://drive.redwood.example/eng-releases/runtime-rollouts

Noah Patel: +1 from runtime. The canary regression evaluator...

text:
Connor O'Brien: putting together the gating checklist for Private releases now that signed offline bundles are required. can folks drop MUST-HAVEs + who signs off? goal is “no cut unless all green” (ties to ENG-4862).

Sahana Iyer: build side: 
- ✅ bundle build is deterministic (same inputs -> same manifest hash) + build logs archived
- ✅ manifest canonicalization step enforced (no pretty-print drift)
- ✅ hashes computed for *every* artifact in bundle (installer, helm chart tgz, image index list, runtime/model artifacts, sbom)
- ✅ CI attestation/provenance generated + stored with release artifacts
sign-off: Build/CI (me) + Release Eng.

Ethan Park: installer gating items:
- ✅ installer preflight verifies signature (fail-closed) on RC bundle
- ✅ `offline-bundle inspect` command returns PASS on RC bundle and prints expected versions/build-id
- ✅ downgrade prevention check passes (signed...

title: #eng-releases: 1760674555
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Kicking off key rotation drill dry-run for offline bundle signing. Goal is to walk the runbook end-to-end (generate new key, update trust store, dual-sign, cut RC, verify installer preflight, capture evidence). Drop notes here as we go.

Sahana Iyer: Here’s the sequence I’m following (from draft runb...
text:
Connor O'Brien: Kicking off key rotation drill dry-run for offline bundle signing. Goal is to walk the runbook end-to-end (generate new key, update trust store, dual-sign, cut RC, verify installer preflight, capture evidence). Drop notes here as we go.

Sahana Iyer: Here’s the sequence I’m following (from draft runbook):
1) create new signing key in HSM slot + export pubkey
2) update installer trust store (ship in next installer build)
3) update CI signing job to dual-sign manifest (`manifest.sig` old + `manifest.sig.new` or combined envelope)
4) verify `rpi bundle inspect --verify` passes with both keys
5) cut a Private RC bundle and test downgrade prevention still works

Devon Price: FYI I pulled the current signing job config from `private-release-signing.yml`. It assumes a single `SIGNING_KEY_ALIAS=offline-bundle-prod` and writes `manifest.sig` only. Dual-sign isn’t wired.

Hanae ...

text:
Connor O'Brien: Kicking off rollout planning for offline bundle signing (Private). Need to lock what lands in which release train + gating checks + owners. Goal is we don’t end up with “signed bundles exist but installer can’t verify” or vice versa.

Talia Benson: +1. Can we pin dates/releases? I’m tracking Phase 1 in the initiative doc but release-train mapping is fuzzy.

Vivek Kulkarni: Product POV: we can do pilot behind “required for air-gapped” first, then make signed bundles the default. But GA criteria should include: verification on by default, clear errors, break-glass path, docs, and support readiness.

Connor O'Brien: Proposal (rough):
- 2025.11 (Private RC / limited): bundle manifest format + CI produces signed artifacts in staging, installer can *inspect* but doesn’t hard-block yet
- 2025.12 (Pilot): installer preflight verifies + fails closed by default, break-glass flag...

text:
Connor O'Brien: Kicking off rollout plan for the new KMS client wrapper stage -> prod. Need owners to sign off on: feature flags, canary criteria, rollback triggers. Targeting first prod canary Monday.

Connor O'Brien: Scope in this rollout: PRs 18421 (wrapper w/ timeouts), 18434 (metrics+tracing), 18449 (circuit breaker + fallback), 18502 (config guardrails), 18515 (error taxonomy fields), 18527 (healthcheck), plus hotfix 18563 already in but we should keep an eye on retry behavior.

Ethan Park: Control-plane side: wrapper is behind `kms.wrapper.enabled` (default false) + dual-read work is separate. For this thread, rollout is just the wrapper + CB policy for config fetch + decrypt paths.

Sean Gallagher: SRE asks: staged rollout by env + region, and by service tier if possible. Also explicit SLO gates: kms latency p95, error rate by category, and overall request 5xx. If we can’t seg...

Connor O'Brien: plan for today: canary quant profiles to 1% of Acme AI traffic. want to lock exact steps + rollback criteria before we flip anything.

Paula Mendes: +1. also pls confirm which route(s) + which profile ref. i need to update the rollout tracker + comms to CS.

Marissa Cole: target = AcmeAI /prod/chat route `acme-prod-chat` only. 1% traffic in us-east first. profile: `qpl://rwq/v1/llama-3.1-70b-instruct/int8_w8a16/calib-2025-02-27@3` (the “stable” one).

Ethan Park: control-plane side is ready. route config supports percentage rollout via policy rule. if we’re doing exactly 1% sampling, we should use request_hash(mod 100) == 0 so it’s deterministic.

Mina Haddad: yep, routing can do deterministic sampling keyed on (tenant_id, route_id, request_id). i’ll paste the rule we used for perf-canary.

Marissa Cole: For enterprise folks reading at 2am: 1) should I upgrade 2) what will break 3) how do I roll back.

- Impact: Optional / Recommended / Mandatory

Connor O'Brien: We need to land on LTS branch/tag naming today-ish. :grimacing: Options being tossed around: `runtime-1.18-lts` branch vs `release/1.18` branch, and tag formats like `1.18.1-lts` vs `v1.18.1+lts`. Main constraint: Private installer + Helm pinning semantics. Anyone have strong opinions / gotchas?

Sah...
text:
Connor O'Brien: We need to land on LTS branch/tag naming today-ish. :grimacing: Options being tossed around: `runtime-1.18-lts` branch vs `release/1.18` branch, and tag formats like `1.18.1-lts` vs `v1.18.1+lts`. Main constraint: Private installer + Helm pinning semantics. Anyone have strong opinions / gotchas?

Sahana Iyer: From CI side: I’d prefer a predictable branch glob we can target in workflows. `runtime-*-lts` is easy. `release/*` is already used by some legacy jobs and I’m worried we’ll accidentally pick up non-LTS release branches.

Bruno Silva: Private installer: we currently parse runtime version as semver in a couple places (upgrade plan + “is upgrade allowed” checks). Suffix `-lts` is technically semver prerelease, which sorts *lower* than the base version.
Bruno Silva: Example: `1.18.1-lts` < `1.18.1` per semver rules. That might be fine, but we need to make sure we nev...

title: #eng-releases: 1740731152
preview:

> New: Opt-in Quantization Profiles (Beta)
> Enable per-route quantization profiles to reduce inference cost while preserving qu...
text:
Connor O'Brien: heads up for next week’s release notes draft — need a decision on wording for Quantization Profile Library + whether we mention it’s behind an allowlist. currently i have:

> New: Opt-in Quantization Profiles (Beta)
> Enable per-route quantization profiles to reduce inference cost while preserving quality and latency. Available for select customers.

thoughts?

Paula Mendes: +1 we should be super explicit it’s opt-in per route and default behavior unchanged. also we should avoid sounding like auto-optimization.

Marissa Cole: Agree. PM ask: do we say “allowlist” or “available to select customers / by request”? allowlist is accurate but tends to trigger a bunch of “how do i get on it” tickets.

Elliot Price: from docs POV we’ve been using “Beta (opt-in, limited availability)” language. “allowlist” is more internal-y.

Alex Martinez: +1. release notes go to a broad audie...

text:
Connor O'Brien: need final eyes on runtime release notes blurb for cache telemetry rollout. goal: accurate + not scary. also confirm feature flag defaults for 1.18.0.

Connor O'Brien: current draft in PR-18435 has:
- “Adds prefix/KV cache hit-rate, eviction pressure, and wasted prefill metrics (opt-in via flags).”
- “New miss reason breakdown (capacity/ttl_expired/policy_disabled/hash_mismatch/version_mismatch).”
- “Sampling + rate limiting for high-volume cache events.”

Connor O'Brien: questions:
1) default flags in prod? staging? dedicated?
2) do we call out dashboards exist (internal) vs Console (not yet)?
3) do we mention metric name change / deprecations now or later?

Omar El-Sayed: for defaults: i strongly prefer OFF by default in prod for first cut, ON in staging + canary. instrumentation is low overhead but we’re still validating ingestion/cardinality in the wild.
